title: CNN This Morning · 2026-06-19 · Nota Kesepahaman AS-Iran dan Kontroversi 'Penyerahan Tanpa Syarat'
summary: Amerika Serikat dan Iran mencapai nota kesepahaman awal, Trump menyebutnya 'penyerahan tanpa syarat', tetapi Iran masih mengendalikan Selat Hormuz dan menetapkan syarat negosiasi. Perundingan damai yang semula dijadwalkan dibatalkan karena konflik antara Israel dan Hizbullah, Wakil Presiden Vance mengkritik pejabat sayap kanan Israel, memicu kontroversi diplomatik. Senator Partai Republik umumnya khawatir dana rekonstruksi sebesar 300 miliar dolar dalam perjanjian itu sebenarnya adalah tebusan terselubung. Cat baru di kolam refleksi Washington mengelupas, memicu diskusi tentang masalah lingkungan.

paragraphs.speaker: Danny Freeman
paragraphs.en: You know, we'll end the morning on a good note. I love it. That does it for CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS. I'm Danny Freeman. CNN THIS MORNING with Audie Cornish starts right now.
paragraphs.cn: Kami akan mengakhiri acara pagi ini dengan cara yang menyenangkan. Saya suka itu. CNN Headline News berakhir di sini. Saya Danny Freeman. Acara CNN 'Pagi Ini', dipandu oleh Otie Cornish, dimulai sekarang.

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: Did Iran surrender to the U.S.? Well, President Trump thinks so.
paragraphs.cn: Apakah Iran menyerah kepada Amerika? Yah, Presiden Trump berpikir begitu.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: The MOU doesn't look like unconditional surrender.
paragraphs.cn: Nota kesepahaman ini tidak terlihat seperti penyerahan tanpa syarat.

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.en: Well, it really probably is unconditional surrender.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, sebenarnya kemungkinan besar itu adalah penyerahan tanpa syarat.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: There's something blue peeling from the bottom of D.C.'s newly painted Reflecting Pool. So, are these new signs of deep trouble in the shallow green water?
paragraphs.cn: Cat biru di dasar kolam refleksi yang baru dicat di Washington, D.C. mengelupas. Jadi, apakah ini tanda-tanda baru masalah di kedalaman perairan hijau muda?

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Let's -- let's be honest. The tone of the administration is not consistently a Christian tone.
paragraphs.cn: Sejujurnya, nada pemerintahan ini tidak selalu bernada Kristen.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: J.D. Vance confronted by a conservative Catholic podcaster, the vice president defending the administration's values. And is Joe Rogan the new Johnny Carson? What America's most popular podcaster has that the King of Late Night never did.
paragraphs.cn: J.D. Vance dihadapkan pada pertanyaan dari seorang podcaster Katolik konservatif, Wakil Presiden membela nilai-nilai pemerintah. Apakah Joe Rogan adalah Johnny Carson yang baru? Apa yang dimiliki podcast paling populer di Amerika yang tidak pernah dimiliki raja acara larut malam.

paragraphs.speaker: J.D. Vance
paragraphs.en: You know, I've seen skeptics of the deal. People say the Iranians will never change their behavior. Well, maybe that's true. And if so, they don't get any of the benefits of the bargain. But isn't it worth trying?
paragraphs.cn: Anda tahu, saya pernah melihat orang yang skeptis terhadap perjanjian itu. Orang-orang mengatakan Iran tidak akan pernah mengubah perilaku mereka. Yah, mungkin itu benar. Jika demikian, mereka tidak akan mendapatkan manfaat apa pun dari perjanjian itu. Tapi bukankah patut dicoba?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The U.S. and Iran giving peace a chance. And yet, just one day into it, could it already be on shaky ground? Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish, and here's where we begin: this breaking news. The peace talks planned today between the U.S. and Iran now called off, at least according to the Swiss foreign minister. Vice President J.D.
paragraphs.cn: Amerika Serikat dan Iran memberi kesempatan pada perdamaian. Namun, hanya sehari kemudian, apakah sudah terancam? Selamat pagi semuanya. Saya Otie Cornish, kita mulai dari sini: berita terbaru ini. Perundingan damai AS-Iran yang dijadwalkan hari ini kini dibatalkan, setidaknya menurut Menteri Luar Negeri Swiss. Wakil Presiden J.D. Vance dijadwalkan pergi ke Swiss tadi malam, tetapi Gedung Putih mengatakan rencananya berubah di menit-menit terakhir karena masalah logistik untuk tahap negosiasi berikutnya belum terselesaikan. Pagi ini, blokade laut telah dicabut. Selat Hormuz secara teknis sudah terbuka. Waktu semakin sempit. Ada 60 hari lagi untuk mencapai kesepakatan akhir. Namun, pernyataan Presiden Trump ini mengejutkan beberapa orang.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Vance was set to travel to Switzerland last night, but the White House said his plans changed last minute, due to unresolved logistics surrounding the next phase of negotiations. This morning, the naval blockade has been lifted. The Strait of Hormuz is technically open. The clock is ticking. Sixty days to get a final deal. President Trump is raising some eyebrows, however, with this.
paragraphs.cn: Vance dijadwalkan pergi ke Swiss tadi malam, tetapi Gedung Putih mengatakan rencananya berubah di menit-menit terakhir karena masalah logistik untuk tahap negosiasi berikutnya belum terselesaikan. Pagi ini, blokade laut telah dicabut. Selat Hormuz secara teknis sudah terbuka. Waktu semakin sempit. Ada 60 hari lagi untuk mencapai kesepakatan akhir. Namun, pernyataan Presiden Trump ini mengejutkan beberapa orang.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: You had talked about you only wanted unconditional surrender and --
paragraphs.cn: Anda pernah mengatakan Anda hanya menginginkan penyerahan tanpa syarat -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: -- the MOU doesn't look like unconditional surrender.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- tetapi nota kesepahaman ini tidak terlihat seperti penyerahan tanpa syarat.

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.en: Well, it really probably is unconditional surrender.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, kemungkinan besar itu sebenarnya adalah penyerahan tanpa syarat.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Joining me now, Kurt Volker, former U.S. ambassador to NATO. Thank you for being here this morning. First, I want to just start with the talks being called off today. Is that bad news? How should we read that?
paragraphs.cn: Sekarang bergabung dengan saya adalah mantan Duta Besar AS untuk NATO, Kurt Volker. Terima kasih telah datang pagi ini. Pertama, saya ingin memulai dengan pembatalan perundingan hari ini. Apakah ini berita buruk? Bagaimana kita harus menafsirkannya?

paragraphs.speaker: Kurt Volker
paragraphs.en: Well, good morning, Audie. And look, I think we had to expect a lot of bumps in the road, because this is a very vague and very partial agreement. It is meant to be the start of negotiations, rather than the end of negotiations. And secondly, it did not bind Israel to anything. It did not bind Hezbollah to anything. So, Iran now has a card to play, which is peace in Lebanon. And if Hezbollah attacks Israel, which apparently happened, then Israel responds, they can claim, OK, there's no peace.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, selamat pagi, Audi. Dengar, saya pikir kita harus mengantisipasi banyak pasang surut, karena ini adalah perjanjian yang sangat kabur dan sangat sepihak. Ini dimaksudkan sebagai awal negosiasi, bukan akhir. Kedua, ini tidak mengikat Israel pada apa pun, juga tidak mengikat Hizbullah pada apa pun. Jadi, Iran sekarang memiliki kartu yang bisa dimainkan, yaitu perdamaian di Lebanon. Jika Hizbullah menyerang Israel -- -- jelas terjadi -- -- maka Israel merespons, dan mereka bisa mengklaim, yah, tidak ada perdamaian.

paragraphs.speaker: Kurt Volker
paragraphs.en: So, we don't have to do anything. So, there's a lot to play out here.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, kita tidak perlu melakukan apa pun. Oleh karena itu, ada banyak variabel di sini.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. You just mentioned that. Last night, at least 16 people, actually, were killed after Israeli airstrikes. Vice President Vance had pretty strong words for members of the Israeli government, who have criticized the agreement with Iran and him. Here's what he had to say.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Anda baru saja menyebutkannya. Tadi malam, setidaknya 16 orang tewas setelah serangan udara Israel. Wakil Presiden Vance mengeluarkan pernyataan yang cukup keras terhadap anggota pemerintah Israel yang mengkritik perjanjian dengan Iran dan dirinya sendiri. Berikut adalah apa yang dia katakan.

paragraphs.speaker: Vance
paragraphs.en: I don't think Bibi himself has actually criticized the deal, because I think he's maybe a little bit more familiar with the details of what's in it. But, yes, you've seen people in their system -- Ben-Gvir and Smotrich -- who've attacked the deal. And I guess my response to them would be, what is your exact proposal? And, you know, you're a country of -- of 9 million people. You can't just kill your way out of solving every single national security problem that you have.
paragraphs.cn: Saya tidak berpikir Bibi sendiri benar-benar mengkritik perjanjian itu, karena saya pikir dia mungkin lebih akrab dengan detail dalam perjanjian itu. Tapi, ya, Anda melihat orang-orang dalam sistem mereka -- -- Ben-Gvir dan Smotrich -- -- menyerang perjanjian itu. Saya pikir tanggapan saya kepada mereka adalah, apa usulan konkret Anda? Dan, Anda tahu, Anda adalah negara dengan 9 juta penduduk. Anda tidak bisa menyelesaikan setiap masalah keamanan nasional hanya dengan membunuh.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: As a diplomat, how do you hear how he's defending this?
paragraphs.cn: Sebagai seorang diplomat, bagaimana Anda memahami pembelaannya seperti itu?

paragraphs.speaker: Volker
paragraphs.en: Well, there are several things that come to mind. No. 1 is that it is an Iranian objective to sow divisions between the United States and Israel. And hearing that kind of comment from the vice president about Israel is fulfilling that Iranian objective of sowing those kinds of divisions. Secondly, if you're Israel, you're living in a very dangerous neighborhood, and what you need to do is provide security for your people. And that rests on deterring what are very real threats to your people.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, beberapa hal terlintas dalam pikiran. Pertama, tujuan Iran adalah menciptakan perpecahan antara Amerika Serikat dan Israel. Mendengar Wakil Presiden membuat komentar seperti itu tentang Israel, justru mencapai tujuan Iran untuk menciptakan perpecahan itu. Kedua, jika Anda adalah Israel, Anda hidup di kawasan yang sangat berbahaya, dan yang perlu Anda lakukan adalah memberikan keamanan bagi rakyat Anda. Dan ini tergantung pada pencegahan terhadap ancaman yang sangat nyata bagi rakyat Anda.

paragraphs.speaker: Volker
paragraphs.en: So, Israel is focused on that. We may not agree with them as to how they go about that. We may think sometimes they are too aggressive in pursuing that. But that is what the Israeli prime minister's job is, and he's not going to be deterred from that. We have to look at that reality. And then thirdly, we shouldn't make an assumption that, because there is now this memorandum of understanding with Iran, that everything that is not in the memorandum of understanding is somehow better than it was before.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, Israel fokus pada hal ini. Kami mungkin tidak setuju dengan cara mereka menangani masalah ini. Kami kadang-kadang mungkin berpikir mereka terlalu agresif dalam mengejar tujuan ini. Tapi itulah tugas Perdana Menteri Israel, dan dia tidak akan terintimidasi oleh hal itu. Kita harus menghadapi kenyataan ini. Ketiga, kita tidak boleh berpikir bahwa hanya karena sekarang kita memiliki nota kesepahaman dengan Iran, segala sesuatu di luar nota kesepahaman itu menjadi lebih baik dari sebelumnya.

paragraphs.speaker: Volker
paragraphs.en: Iran funds, supplies, trains, supports terrorist networks all over the world, especially those that are targeting Israel. And we have to recognize that that is real.
paragraphs.cn: Iran mendanai, memasok, melatih, dan mendukung jaringan teror di seluruh dunia, terutama yang menargetkan Israel. Kita harus menyadari bahwa ini adalah fakta.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. And I think a lot of people do, frankly. The people who are loudest in their complaints about this deal are Republicans. And we're just hearing so many senators saying similar things, even, to what you might hear from those loud voices in Israel. I want to play for you a series of voices. SEN. JOHN CORNYN (R-TX): Everything I've heard about it causes me concern. SEN. TOM COTTON (R-AR): I do have concerns that certain aspects of this deal might be a step in the wrong direction. SEN.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Dan saya pikir banyak orang memang begitu. Yang paling keras mengeluh tentang perjanjian ini adalah Partai Republik. Kami mendengar banyak senator mengatakan hal serupa, bahkan mirip dengan suara-suara keras yang Anda dengar di Israel. Saya ingin memutar serangkaian suara untuk Anda. Senator John Cornyn (R-TX): Semua yang saya dengar membuat saya khawatir. Senator Tom Cotton (R-AR): Saya memang khawatir beberapa aspek perjanjian ini mungkin merupakan langkah ke arah yang salah.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: JONI ERNST (R-IA): I have to know where that money is coming from, because I don't think my constituents are going to be really happy about it, if that's all U.S. taxpayer dollars. SEN. TED CRUZ (R-TX): History demonstrates that giving billions of dollars to theocratic lunatics who want to murder us is an exceptionally bad idea.
paragraphs.cn: Senator Joni Ernst (Republican from Iowa): Saya harus tahu dari mana uang ini berasal, karena jika semuanya dari uang pajak Amerika, saya rasa pemilih saya tidak akan senang. Senator Ted Cruz (Republican from Texas): Sejarah menunjukkan bahwa memberikan miliaran dolar kepada orang gila teokratis yang ingin membunuh kita adalah ide yang sangat buruk.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, people are kind of up in arms about many things, but specifically, the $300 billion potential reconstruction and development fund, which is supposed to be an investment fund. And Sen. Roger Wicker of Mississippi is the top Republican. He was saying, Look, I get that it's not funded by taxpayer dollars, but it's still a payoff. And he's saying it's a payoff that would make Obama's 2015 deal look like a pittance by comparison.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, orang marah tentang banyak hal, tetapi secara spesifik, dana rekonstruksi dan pembangunan potensial sebesar $300 miliar, yang seharusnya menjadi dana investasi. Senator Roger Wicker dari Mississippi adalah petinggi Partai Republik. Dia berkata, saya paham ini tidak didanai oleh uang pajak, tapi ini tetap suap. Dia juga berkata, dibandingkan dengan suap ini, kesepakatan Obama 2015 tidak ada apa-apanya.

paragraphs.speaker: Volker
paragraphs.en: Yes, that's exactly right. That's the one part of this thing that is the most mystifying at all: why there's any money changing hands here, whether it's taxpayers or not. It doesn't matter.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, benar sekali. Ini bagian paling membingungkan dari semuanya: mengapa ada dana yang berpindah tangan di sini, entah dari pajak atau tidak. Itu tidak penting.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But they're saying there isn't, right? They're arguing that money isn't changing hands.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi mereka bilang tidak, kan? Mereka berargumen tidak ada dana yang berpindah tangan.

paragraphs.speaker: Volker
paragraphs.en: Well, they say that, but then you look, and you say sanctions are being lifted. Iran is getting oil sales. There will be a reconstruction fund that the Gulf states will organize. That's money. And what you have to look at here is where we were in January, was Iran's nuclear program had been bombed and substantially destroyed. And the Gulf [SIC] of Hormuz was open. Now, we had four months of war that we paid for, and the nuclear program remains destroyed.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, mereka bilang begitu, tapi lihat, sanksi sedang dicabut. Iran mendapatkan penjualan minyak. Negara-negara Teluk akan mengatur dana rekonstruksi. Itulah uangnya. Anda harus melihat bahwa pada Januari tahun ini, program nuklir Iran telah dibom dan pada dasarnya hancur. Selat Hormuz terbuka. Sekarang, kita telah melalui perang empat bulan, kita membayar untuk itu, dan program nuklir masih hancur.

paragraphs.speaker: Volker
paragraphs.en: And we are still in an open-ended negotiation about where that goes. And there is some Iranian insistence that they have some control over the state of -- the Strait of Hormuz. OK. But maybe it was worth it to get out of the military conflict, to get our forces home, to bring down oil prices, to restore some stability in the global economy. You can argue, OK, we can go back and reset. But then you add on top of that, and Iran gets access to hundreds of billions of dollars. That's kind of inexplicable.
paragraphs.cn: Kita masih dalam negosiasi tanpa akhir tentang arah situasi. Iran bersikeras memiliki kendali tertentu atas kondisi Selat Hormuz. Baiklah. Tapi mungkin keluar dari konflik militer, membawa pulang pasukan kita, menurunkan harga minyak, dan memulihkan stabilitas ekonomi global itu sepadan. Anda bisa bilang, oke, kita bisa kembali dan memulai lagi. Tapi di atas itu, Iran masih bisa mendapatkan ratusan miliar dolar. Ini agak sulit dijelaskan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, none of this sounds like what the president said: unconditional surrender by Iran.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, semua ini tidak terdengar seperti apa yang dikatakan Presiden: Iran menyerah tanpa syarat.

paragraphs.speaker: Volker
paragraphs.en: Well, I know that's what he wants to say, but the Iranian regime is still in place. They are setting terms over the Strait of Hormuz. They are setting terms for future negotiations about nuclear weapons. And as we saw today, they're also insisting on demands about Lebanon that is mentioned in the agreement but has no binding effect on Israel or Hezbollah.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, saya tahu apa yang ingin dia katakan, tapi rezim Iran masih ada. Mereka menetapkan kondisi di Selat Hormuz. Mereka menetapkan kondisi untuk negosiasi masa depan tentang senjata nuklir. Seperti yang kita lihat hari ini, mereka juga bersikeras pada tuntutan di Lebanon, yang disebutkan dalam kesepakatan, tapi tidak mengikat Israel atau Hizbullah.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: That's Ambassador Kurt Volker. Thank you so much for your time.
paragraphs.cn: Ini Duta Besar Kurt Volker. Terima kasih banyak telah meluangkan waktu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Coming up on CNN THIS MORNING, accused killer Luigi Mangione changes his mind. His lawyers announcing another new defense strategy this week. And the Reflecting Pool here on the National Mall got a multimillion- dollar makeover. But does President Trump's fix now need some fixing? Plus, today is Juneteenth, and the Juneteenth flag is now flying over Wisconsin's state capitol for the seventh year in a row. We'll be right back.
paragraphs.cn: Di acara CNN This Morning, tersangka pembunuh Luigi Mangione berubah pikiran. Pengacaranya mengumumkan strategi pembelaan baru lainnya minggu ini. Reflecting Pool di National Mall menjalani renovasi senilai jutaan dolar. Tapi apakah perbaikan Presiden Trump sekarang perlu diperbaiki? Juga, hari ini adalah Juneteenth, bendera Juneteenth berkibar di atas Wisconsin State Capitol untuk tahun ketujuh berturut-turut. Kami segera kembali.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: It looks bad. I just see green. Green slime.
paragraphs.cn: Kelihatannya mengerikan. Saya hanya melihat hijau. Lendir hijau.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: Pouring all that peroxide into it clearly didn't help. I feel for the ducks.
paragraphs.cn: Menuangkan hidrogen peroksida sebanyak itu jelas tidak membantu. Saya kasihan pada bebek-bebek itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right. We could call it "Watergate." A new issue after the multi-million-dollar renovation of the Reflecting Pool. Now we're going to show you this: blue material peeling off the bottom. It's not clear yet if that's paint or sealant. And earlier this week, crews were working on the algae turning the pool green. Despite these images, the U.S. Department of the Interior insists that the water is crystal-clear and that crews are just vacuuming up dead algae.
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah. Kita bisa menyebutnya "Watergate." Masalah baru setelah renovasi kolam refleksi senilai jutaan dolar. Sekarang kami akan tunjukkan ini: bahan biru terkelupas dari dasarnya. Belum jelas apakah itu cat atau sealant. Awal minggu ini, petugas sedang menangani alga yang membuat kolam menjadi hijau. Meskipun ada gambar-gambar ini, Departemen Dalam Negeri AS bersikeras airnya jernih dan petugas hanya membersihkan alga mati.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Quote, "just like the destroyed Iranian navy resting on the bottom of the Persian Gulf." Again, that's the word from the Department of the Interior in charge of the Reflecting Pool. Joining me now in the group chat, Elliot Williams, CNN legal analyst and former federal prosecutor; Michael Warren, politics editor for "The Dispatch"; and Lulu Garcia-Navarro, CNN contributor and host of "The New York Times" podcast, "The Interview." All right.
paragraphs.cn: Kutipan, "Seperti angkatan laut Iran yang hancur terbaring di dasar Teluk Persia." Sekali lagi, ini dari Departemen Dalam Negeri yang bertanggung jawab atas kolam refleksi. Bergabung dalam obrolan grup kami: analis hukum CNN, mantan jaksa federal Elliot Williams; editor politik The Telegraph Michael Warren; dan kontributor CNN, pembawa acara podcast The New York Times The Interview, Lulu Garcia-Navarro. Oke.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, both the Reflecting Pool and the Iran war are a win, which if you were here for my interview with Ambassador Volker just now, I'm not sure that's a great analogy. But I want to point out: at least two companies worked on this project. And public records show that an Ohio company, Greenwater Services, was hired to install the filtration system, and a Virginia- based Atlantic Industrial Coatings to resurface it.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, kolam refleksi dan perang Iran sama-sama kemenangan, jika Anda baru saja mendengar wawancara saya dengan Duta Besar Volker, saya tidak yakin itu analogi yang bagus. Tapi saya ingin menunjukkan: setidaknya dua perusahaan terlibat dalam proyek ini. Catatan publik menunjukkan Greenwater Services dari Ohio dipekerjakan untuk memasang sistem filtrasi, dan Atlantic Industrial Coatings dari Virginia untuk melapisi ulang permukaan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And even though Greenwater Services didn't respond to our request for comment, the owner of Atlantic Industrial Coatings did. They said -- Eddie Wood, he's -- who owns Atlantic Industrial Coatings, said, "The images do not provide enough information to tell exactly what this is. There are several things that we've got to address when we come back for maintenance, and anything like that will be addressed if it's a problem."
paragraphs.cn: Meskipun Greenwater Services tidak menanggapi permintaan komentar kami, pemilik Atlantic Industrial Coatings merespons. Mereka berkata -- -- Eddie Wood, pemilik Atlantic Industrial Coatings, berkata: "Gambar-gambar itu tidak memberikan informasi yang cukup untuk menentukan dengan pasti apa ini. Ada beberapa hal yang perlu ditangani saat kami kembali untuk pemeliharaan, dan jika ada masalah, hal serupa akan ditangani."

paragraphs.speaker: Lulu Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: So first of all, the fact that it was called "Greenwater" should have been a giveaway. I'm just saying you can't make that up. Sorry. Go on. MICHAEL WARREN, POLITICS EDITOR, "THE DISPATCH": No. No,
paragraphs.cn: Jadi pertama, disebut "Greenwater" seharusnya menjadi petunjuk. Saya hanya mengatakan ini bukan dibuat-buat. Maaf. Silakan lanjutkan. Michael Warren, editor politik The Telegraph: Tidak. Tidak,

paragraphs.speaker: I -- Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: I had to make the joke.
paragraphs.cn: Saya harus melontarkan lelucon itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Elliot Williams
paragraphs.en: Delivered well. It was good.
paragraphs.cn: Lelucon bagus. Cukup bagus.

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: No, look, I -- I think this is one of these things that was thoroughly predictable, if you read anything, which you know, I know nothing about water filtration. But I was reading about this as the administration was pushing this narrative that they were finally cleaning it up. Is that this is a long-standing problem in the Reflecting Pool, that these algae blooms happen, because there just isn't enough, you know, water flow out of -- out of it.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, lihat, saya pikir ini sepenuhnya bisa diprediksi, jika Anda membaca liputan apa pun tentang ini -- -- Anda tahu, saya tidak tahu apa-apa tentang filtrasi air. Tapi saya membacanya ketika pemerintah mempromosikan bahwa mereka akhirnya akan membersihkan kolam. Ini masalah lama kolam refleksi, alga mekar karena aliran air tidak memadai.

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: And I do think that, you know, two weeks ago, a week ago on social media, you had all these kind of supporters of the president, Republicans saying, finally, the president is cleaning this up. And it was just -- it was obvious that this was going --
paragraphs.cn: Dan saya memang berpikir, dua minggu lalu, seminggu lalu di media sosial, para pendukung presiden, Partai Republik, mengatakan presiden akhirnya akan membersihkan kolam ini. Jelas ini akan -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: This was very likely to happen. Now, maybe they're just cleaning up dead algae, maybe just the seal is coming off. That doesn't --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yeah, and also there's male fans. Like, I remember during the "Heated Rivalry" thing, like, all the hockey guys who got into that show, which was, you know, a vibe.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, dan ada juga penggemar pria. Misalnya, saya ingat dalam kasus "Persaingan Ketat", semua pemain hoki es itu tergila-gila dengan acara itu, kamu tahu, itu semacam suasana.

paragraphs.speaker: Lulu Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: It's for everyone. I mean, I think this is a tale as old as time, frankly. I mean, where do you get these stories? And -- and --
paragraphs.cn: Ini untuk semua orang. Maksud saya, terus terang, saya pikir ini adalah cerita lama. Maksud saya, dari mana datangnya cerita-cerita ini? Dan -- -- dan -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But romance novels were sort of in the ghetto of genres. Like, people were like, ugh.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi novel roman dulu adalah ghetto dalam genre. Seperti, orang akan merasa, uh.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: They were. They were, but I think that that was a misunderstanding. As a longtime romance reader and a longtime reader of romantasy --
paragraphs.cn: Dulu begitu, tapi saya pikir itu kesalahpahaman. Sebagai pembaca novel roman jangka panjang dan pembaca roman fantasi jangka panjang -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Are you pounding the table right now? (LAUGHTER)
paragraphs.cn: Apakah kamu sekarang memukul meja? (Tawa)

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: I am because I feel very strongly and I'm very invested in this. Female rage is something that doesn't get enough stuff. (CROSSTALK)
paragraphs.cn: Ya, karena perasaanku sangat kuat, dan aku sangat terlibat di dalamnya. Kemarahan perempuan tidak mendapat perhatian yang cukup. (Percakapan silang)

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: But you know, I do think that this is a genre that gets pooh-poohed by in elite circles, but has always been the engine of publishing. Women read. Women are the biggest readers, book clubs. And these are the stories that, you know, that we enjoy for all sorts of reasons.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi kau tahu, aku memang berpikir ini adalah genre yang dicemooh di kalangan elit, tapi selalu menjadi mesin penerbitan. Perempuan membaca. Perempuan adalah kelompok pembaca terbesar, dan klub buku. Dan cerita-cerita ini, kau tahu, kita menyukainya karena berbagai alasan.

paragraphs.speaker: Shapiro
paragraphs.en: But we have seen Hollywood as invested as they are now.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi kita melihat Hollywood berinvestasi seperti sekarang.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: I agree, but I don't think they understood how to make them in the way that you get the nuance in the books, right? In the books, you're getting character development, and then it all would get flattened off into Harlequin romance type stuff. The big subtext here is the social media is everything, right? I don't know if we wouldn't be here at all without these folks on TikTok. But again, you're talking about literally tens of millions of people promoting books and sharing them and so on. There we go.
paragraphs.cn: Aku setuju, tapi kupikir mereka tidak tahu cara membuatnya dengan menangkap nuansa buku, kan? Di buku, kau melihat perkembangan karakter, lalu semua itu diratakan menjadi novel roman murahan. Latar belakang besarnya adalah media sosial adalah segalanya, kan? Tanpa orang-orang di TikTok ini, aku tidak tahu apakah kita masih akan di sini. Tapi sekali lagi, kau bicara tentang puluhan juta orang yang mempromosikan buku, berbagi buku, dan sebagainya. Itu saja.

paragraphs.speaker: Michael Warren
paragraphs.en: What's old is new again. Sex sells. This is, like, a shocking idea.
paragraphs.cn: Anggur lama dalam botol baru. Seksi menarik. Itu ide yang menakjubkan.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: It's not just sex. It's the romance.
paragraphs.cn: Bukan hanya seks, tapi juga romansa.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And we end the segment with the men arguing about how much sex is in the romance genre. Ari, thank you so much for being here.
paragraphs.cn: Kita akhiri segmen ini dengan pria yang berdebat tentang berapa banyak konten seks dalam genre romansa. Ari, terima kasih banyak sudah datang.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Love it. We've got new episodes of "Engagement Party" every Friday. Check us out. And in the meantime, we're going to lawyer up. Elliot's talking about guns and marijuana, why this latest Supreme Court decision could reach far beyond one man's case, and today's talks with Iran, the formal signing ceremony that's been canceled at the last minute.
paragraphs.cn: Luar biasa. Pesta Pertunangan kami punya episode baru setiap hari Jumat. Ikuti kami. Sementara itu, kami akan mencari pengacara. Elliot bicara tentang senjata dan ganja, mengapa putusan terbaru Mahkamah Agung mungkin jauh melampaui kasus satu orang, dan pembicaraan hari ini dengan Iran, upacara penandatanganan resmi dibatalkan pada menit terakhir.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. I want to thank you for joining me on CNN THIS MORNING. It is half past the hour. And we're following this breaking news. Overnight, today's planned talks between Iran and the U.S. canceled. Vice President delaying his trip to Switzerland at the last minute because of what the White House is calling unresolved logistics surrounding the next phase of negotiations. And U.S. boat strikes on suspected drug-smuggling boats still happening.
paragraphs.cn: Selamat pagi semuanya. Aku Audie Cornish. Terima kasih telah menonton CNN Pagi Ini. Sekarang pukul setengah. Kami sedang mengikuti berita terbaru ini. Tadi malam, pertemuan yang dijadwalkan hari ini antara Iran dan AS dibatalkan. Wakil Presiden menunda perjalanannya ke Swiss pada menit terakhir, dan Gedung Putih mengatakan alasannya adalah masalah logistik untuk tahap negosiasi berikutnya belum terselesaikan. Serangan AS terhadap kapal yang diduga penyelundup narkoba terus berlanjut.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The latest was yesterday, and three people were killed. At least 211 people have been killed in the Trump administration's war on alleged drug boats. Some lawmakers are calling for an investigation, saying the administration is not offering enough evidence to support its claims. And today, on Juneteenth, the Obama Presidential Center is set to open to the public. Yesterday, the Obamas celebrated the grand opening. Former Presidents Clinton, Bush, and Biden were there to support.
paragraphs.cn: Yang terbaru terjadi kemarin, menewaskan tiga orang. Dalam perang pemerintahan Trump terhadap kapal yang diduga penyelundup narkoba, setidaknya 211 orang tewas. Beberapa anggota parlemen menyerukan penyelidikan, mengatakan pemerintah tidak memberikan cukup bukti untuk mendukung klaimnya. Hari ini, pada Hari Juneteenth, Pusat Presiden Obama akan dibuka untuk umum. Kemarin, keluarga Obama merayakan pembukaan besar. Mantan Presiden Clinton, Bush, dan Obama hadir untuk mendukung.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And the former first lady kicked off the celebration honoring what she called her husband's dedication to the American people.
paragraphs.cn: Mantan Ibu Negara memulai perayaan untuk menghormati apa yang dia sebut pengabdian suaminya kepada rakyat Amerika.

paragraphs.speaker: Michelle Obama
paragraphs.en: Yeah, you were unflappable at every turn, always focused, always calm, always looking at the long view.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, kau tenang di setiap titik, selalu fokus, selalu tenang, selalu berpikir jangka panjang.

paragraphs.speaker: Barack Obama
paragraphs.en: Democracy can be frustrating. It can be slow. It can be inefficient. And yet, more than anything, I hope this center will serve as an affirmation of just how special, how precious our democracy truly is.
paragraphs.cn: Demokrasi kadang membuat frustrasi. Bisa lambat. Bisa tidak efisien. Namun yang terpenting, aku berharap pusat ini bisa membuktikan betapa istimewa dan berharganya demokrasi kita.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now, general admission tickets for the center are sold out through the end of October. And the Supreme Court expanding gun rights to some who light up. The High Court siding with a Texas man who argued that he shouldn't be criminally charged for owning a gun just because he smokes marijuana. The decision, loosening a federal ban, but the ruling is not so cut and dry, which is why it's time to lawyer up with CNN legal analyst Elliot Williams. Okay, you started out a baby federal prosecutor once upon a time.
paragraphs.cn: Saat ini, tiket umum untuk pusat itu sudah habis terjual hingga akhir Oktober. Mahkamah Agung memperluas hak kepemilikan senjata bagi sebagian pengguna narkoba. Pengadilan mendukung argumen seorang pria Texas yang mengatakan dia tidak seharusnya didakwa secara pidana karena memiliki senjata hanya karena dia menggunakan ganja. Putusan ini melonggarkan larangan federal, tetapi keputusannya tidak bersifat mutlak, jadi saatnya memanggil analis hukum CNN Elliot Williams untuk menjelaskannya. Baik, kau dulu adalah jaksa federal junior.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Baby federal prosecutor once upon a time.
paragraphs.cn: Dulu adalah jaksa federal junior.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And I kind of think of prosecutors liking a good drug charge here and there.
paragraphs.cn: Aku agak merasa jaksa suka sesekali menangani tuduhan narkoba.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: And one of the more common things that young prosecutors prosecute is this kind of case, which is a prohibition case where an individual is prohibited from owning a firearm based on the class of society that they belong to. The big ones are felons that's a very common one, but also drug users someone who is a drug user or an addict of any controlled substance can be barred from owning a firearm.
paragraphs.cn: Salah satu kasus yang sering dituntut jaksa muda adalah kasus larangan, di mana seseorang dilarang memiliki senjata karena kelas sosialnya. Yang paling umum adalah penjahat, tapi ada juga pengguna narkoba -- -- setiap pengguna atau pecandu zat terlarang bisa dilarang memiliki senjata.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Well, this individual who was a casual habitual marijuana user said he used it a few times a week had his firearm taken away and brought a lawsuit saying that -- well, the Second Amendment should not bar someone like me from using a firearm.
paragraphs.cn: Pria ini adalah pengguna ganja sesekali dan kebiasaan, dia bilang dia pakai beberapa kali seminggu, senjatanya disita, lalu dia menggugat, mengklaim Amandemen Kedua tidak boleh melarang orang seperti dia menggunakan senjata.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Can you talk about how this is complicated by the moment we're in or recreation racial marijuana is everywhere? I don't even know how you define a casual habitual user.
paragraphs.cn: Bisakah kau bicara bagaimana ini menjadi rumit di zaman kita atau dengan maraknya ganja rekreasi? Aku bahkan tidak tahu bagaimana mendefinisikan pengguna sesekali dan kebiasaan.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Exactly right. And that was exactly -- so it's a unanimous decision, but Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson picked up on that point exactly. She says that the state of our jurisprudence on firearms leaves open these open questions. So how do you define what a casual user of marijuana is? Now, the way they use it is that, number one, it is increasingly in a different class of substance than cocaine, heroin, whatever else, and it just had its classifications level lower. That's what Justice Gorsuch said.
paragraphs.cn: Tepat sekali. Itulah -- -- jadi ini putusan bulat, tapi Hakim Ketanji Brown Jackson justru menunjukkan hal itu. Dia bilang, hukum kasus kita tentang senjata meninggalkan pertanyaan yang belum terjawab ini. Jadi, bagaimana mendefinisikan pengguna ganja sesekali? Cara mereka menggunakan, pertama, ini semakin termasuk dalam kategori zat yang berbeda dari kokain, heroin, dll., dan peringkat klasifikasinya baru saja diturunkan. Itu yang dikatakan Hakim Gorsuch.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: But it's a vague standard. And I just think what we're seeing here is the convergence of two big areas in American law, relaxing laws on cannabis, but also relaxing laws on firearms. And the Supreme Court has been heading in that direction in both areas, and they really came to a head.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi ini standar yang kabur. Kupikir yang kita lihat di sini adalah pertemuan dua bidang besar hukum AS: pelonggaran hukum ganja, sekaligus pelonggaran hukum senjata. Mahkamah Agung bergerak ke arah itu di kedua bidang, dan sekarang mereka benar-benar bertabrakan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yeah. And so that's the legal part. The political bedfellows of this is also, it's just like really interesting because I think even in Republican circles, there is, let's say, a more relaxed attitude in so many ways towards cannabis for sure, but even the punishment aspect of that.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, itu dari sisi hukum. Sekutu politik juga menarik, karena kupikir bahkan di dalam Partai Republik, sikap terhadap ganja dalam banyak hal lebih longgar, termasuk soal hukuman.

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: And look, I think that if you look at all the different, you know, the opinions and then the concurrences and you had different viewpoints all coming to the same conclusion and I think that is reflected in the fact that it was an 9-0 decision. And it's a reminder as well that a lot of controversial or supposedly controversial issues that there can be and there often is in in the Supreme Court, a lot of nine or eight decisions.
paragraphs.cn: Dan, kupikir jika kau lihat semua pendapat dan persetujuan yang berbeda, sudut pandang yang berbeda mencapai kesimpulan yang sama, yang tercermin dalam putusan 9-0. Ini juga mengingatkan kita bahwa banyak isu kontroversial atau yang dianggap kontroversial, seringkali di Mahkamah Agung punya putusan 9-0 atau 8-1.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The more shocking thing.
paragraphs.cn: Lebih mengejutkan lagi.

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: No, but I think it's actually it's less shocking than if you follow the court and look at a lot of the reasoning. I mean, even Elena Kagan, a liberal appointed justice, as you know, we're all originalists now, as she said several years ago. I think that there is oftentimes those don't get as much attention. But on an issue like this, I think it does get attention because --
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, saya pikir jika Anda memperhatikan pengadilan dan memeriksa penalarannya, sebenarnya tidak terlalu mengejutkan. Bahkan Hakim Elena Kagan yang ditunjuk oleh kaum liberal, seperti yang dia katakan beberapa tahun lalu, kita semua sekarang adalah originalis. Saya pikir hal-hal ini sering tidak mendapat perhatian yang cukup. Tapi dalam masalah seperti ini, hal itu memang menarik perhatian, karena --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Can I add this legal footnote?
paragraphs.cn: Bisakah saya menambahkan catatan kaki hukum?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: This is the same law used against Hunter Biden --
paragraphs.cn: Hukum yang sama juga digunakan untuk Hunter Biden --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- when he was convicted of buying a gun while being a cocaine user in 2018. Now, of course, he was pardoned for all the things.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- Dia dihukum pada tahun 2018 karena membeli senjata api sebagai pengguna kokain. Tentu saja, dia kemudian diampuni dari semua kejahatan.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: There's a few things --
paragraphs.cn: Ada beberapa hal --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But it's interesting that this law has been deployed in a very public way.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi yang menarik adalah bahwa hukum ini digunakan dengan cara yang sangat publik.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Yeah, there's a few things going on here. One, to your point, it's the strange bedfellows of pro-gun folks, but also people with different types of drug convictions. Now, they're very careful to say this is just for casual users of marijuana, other people with different sort of substances will still have the prohibition apply to them. I think the Supreme Court was trying to find a way to carve marijuana out of the law. Like the other prohibitions, like being a felon, either you are or you aren't.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, ada beberapa hal di sini. Pertama, seperti yang Anda katakan, ini adalah aliansi aneh antara pendukung kepemilikan senjata dan mereka yang dihukum karena narkoba jenis berbeda. Mereka sangat hati-hati mengatakan bahwa ini hanya berlaku untuk pengguna ganja sesekali, pengguna zat lain yang berbeda masih akan tunduk pada larangan. Saya pikir Mahkamah Agung mencoba menemukan cara untuk mengeluarkan ganja dari hukum. Dan larangan lain, seperti untuk penjahat, entah ya atau tidak.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Either you have a conviction or you do not. Here, it's a vague standard of how do you ultimately define when someone is sufficiently dangerous based on the type of substance they're using. And it is a pretty vague standard that they follow.
paragraphs.cn: Entah Anda memiliki hukuman atau tidak. Di sini, mereka mendasarkan pada standar yang kabur, yaitu bagaimana berdasarkan jenis zat yang digunakan seseorang pada akhirnya menentukan apakah mereka cukup berbahaya. Standar yang mereka ikuti cukup kabur.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right, you guys stay here. Next, we're going to talk about something else. There's a former late-night host with a hot take. We'll talk about why Jay Leno is saying that podcasters are the new king of talk. Plus, there's this.
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah, kalian tetap di sini. Selanjutnya, kita akan membahas hal lain. Seorang mantan pembawa acara larut malam menyampaikan pandangan tajam. Mari kita bahas mengapa Jay Leno mengatakan podcaster adalah raja talk show baru. Selain itu, ada ini.

paragraphs.speaker: J.D. Vance
paragraphs.en: I'm not saying we're perfect because we're not.
paragraphs.cn: Saya tidak mengatakan kita sempurna, karena kita tidak sempurna.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: An interview, the fellow Catholic takes a turn. Vice President Vance pressed on whether the administration practices what it preaches.
paragraphs.cn: Dalam sebuah wawancara, pernyataan pria Katolik ini berbelok. Wakil Presiden Vance ditanya apakah pemerintah konsisten antara kata dan perbuatan.

paragraphs.speaker: Ross Douthat
paragraphs.en: Let's be honest, the tone of the administration is not consistently a Christian tone. There is a tone of aggressive uncharity to people who aren't on board with the administration's policies.
paragraphs.cn: Sejujurnya, nada pemerintahan ini tidak selalu bernada Kristen. Ada sikap tidak ramah yang agresif terhadap mereka yang tidak setuju dengan kebijakan pemerintah.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So Vice President Vance sat down for what he probably thought might be a friendly interview with Catholic conservative writer Ross Douthat, but the administration's Christian values were quickly called into question. And here's how Vance pushed back.
paragraphs.cn: Maka Wakil Presiden Vance duduk untuk wawancara yang mungkin dia kira akan bersahabat, dengan penulis konservatif Katolik Ross Douthat, tetapi nilai-nilai Kristen pemerintah segera dipertanyakan. Berikut adalah bantahan Vance.

paragraphs.speaker: Vance
paragraphs.en: For every clip that you could show me of me or the president or some cabinet secretary saying something that, in your view, is unchristian, I could show you another few clips of us doing something or saying something that is like very Christian. I'm not saying we're perfect because we're not.
paragraphs.cn: Untuk setiap video yang bisa Anda tunjukkan kepada saya, yang menunjukkan saya, Presiden, atau seorang menteri kabinet mengatakan sesuatu yang Anda anggap tidak Kristen, saya bisa menunjukkan beberapa video lain yang menunjukkan kami melakukan atau mengatakan hal yang sangat Kristen. Saya tidak mengatakan kita sempurna, karena kita tidak sempurna.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Group chat is back. I wanted to talk about this. Michael, you had like a pretty interesting take where you found his answers evasive, which is interesting, but also a poor Christian witness.
paragraphs.cn: Obrolan grup kembali. Saya ingin membahas ini. Michael, Anda memiliki pandangan yang cukup menarik bahwa jawabannya menghindari pertanyaan, itu menarik, tetapi juga merupakan kesaksian Kristen yang buruk.

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: Look, if I were to walk into my confessional and tell the priest, oh, I've committed these sins, but you wouldn't believe all the great stuff that I was doing as well.
paragraphs.cn: Dengar, jika saya masuk ke ruang pengakuan dan berkata kepada pastor, oh, saya telah melakukan dosa-dosa ini, tetapi Anda tidak akan percaya saya juga melakukan semua hal baik itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: It doesn't work. I never tried it because I'm afraid.
paragraphs.cn: Itu tidak akan berhasil. Saya belum pernah mencobanya, karena saya takut.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You don't get fewer Hail Marys --
paragraphs.cn: Anda tidak akan mengurangi Salam Maria --

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: No, no, no. In fact, I'd probably get a lot more. Look, I think, you know, I can't know what's in JD Vance's heart. I just think --
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, tidak, tidak. Sebenarnya, saya mungkin akan menambahkannya. Dengar, saya pikir, Anda tahu, saya tidak bisa mengetahui isi hati J.D. Vance. Saya hanya merasa --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Well, he purports to tell us. I mean, he's on a book tour called for this book called "Communion" --
paragraphs.cn: Ya, dia mengaku memberitahu kita. Maksud saya, dia sedang melakukan tur promosi untuk buku berjudul "Ekaristi" ini --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- which he is trying to show his heart.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- Dia mencoba menunjukkan hatinya.

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: Well, and I think that's admirable. And I know that, you know, as myself, as a work in progress, Christian and Catholic, that it is something you have to do every single day. But I find the sort of dismissal of the question about tone, of the words that the administration uses, and even J.D. Vance himself, I've written about this from the beginning of this administration --
paragraphs.cn: Ya, saya pikir itu patut dihargai. Dan saya tahu, sebagai seorang Kristen dan Katolik, saya sendiri terus berkembang, itu adalah sesuatu yang harus Anda lakukan setiap hari. Tapi saya menemukan bahwa untuk nada, bahasa yang digunakan pemerintah, bahkan J.D. Vance sendiri, sejak awal saya telah menulis tentang pemerintahan ini --

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: -- toward the U.S. Conference of Catholic Bishops, about the sort of immigration policy, that the words matter, the sort of things that we do every single day.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- terhadap Konferensi Waligereja Amerika Serikat, tentang kebijakan imigrasi, kata-kata itu penting, dan hal-hal yang kita lakukan setiap hari juga penting.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: It's the words that matter. It's the policies that matter.
paragraphs.cn: Yang penting adalah kata-kata. Yang penting adalah kebijakan.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: I mean, I think my colleague Ross Douthat, that an opinion was obviously pressing on what is -- what are Christian values. And that becomes central why, because this is an administration that really wears its Christianity front and center controversially. You know, many are worried that they are trying to push a kind of Christian nationalism in this country that we traditionally have not seen. And so when we see J.D.
paragraphs.cn: Maksud saya, saya pikir sebuah opini dari rekan saya, Ross Douthat, jelas menekankan apa itu nilai-nilai Kristen. Ini menjadi krusial karena pemerintahan ini secara kontroversial menempatkan Kristen di garis depan dan pusat. Anda tahu, banyak orang khawatir bahwa mereka mencoba untuk menerapkan nasionalisme Kristen di negara ini yang belum pernah kita lihat secara tradisional. Jadi ketika kita melihat J.D.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Vance sort of front his religion and his own religious journey, and, of course, you know, I've interviewed him about his conversion to Catholicism. he wears his faith very -- you know, I believe in the sincerity of his faith, but those public expressions of that are important politically. Why? And what do we mean when we understand Christian values?
paragraphs.cn: Vance menunjukkan agamanya dan perjalanan agamanya, tentu saja, Anda tahu, saya mewawancarainya tentang pertobatannya ke Katolik. Dia sangat terbuka tentang imannya -- saya percaya pada ketulusan imannya, tetapi ekspresi publik ini penting secara politis. Mengapa? Ketika kita memahami nilai-nilai Kristen, apa yang kita maksud?

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: And this has become an issue with the pope himself.
paragraphs.cn: Ini telah menjadi masalah yang terkait dengan Paus sendiri.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Exactly. And was an issue with the prior pope. One of the things I learned in researching this is that when J.D. Vance made this comment about the sort of the order of love, like kind of ranking the things you should care about, citing Catholic doctrine, the letter and the response from Pope Francis was actually penned by a person who became Pope Leo. So, he's well-acquainted, right, with the real response from the Vatican in terms of outlining these things publicly.
paragraphs.cn: Benar. Dan ini juga terkait dengan Paus sebelumnya. Saat meneliti ini, saya mengetahui bahwa ketika J.D. Vance mengomentari doktrin Katolik tentang tatanan cinta -- yaitu urutan hal-hal yang harus Anda pedulikan -- surat balasan Paus Fransiskus sebenarnya ditulis oleh orang yang kemudian menjadi Paus Leo. Jadi, dia sangat sadar akan tanggapan nyata Vatikan ketika membahas masalah-masalah ini secara publik.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Right. I think what we're seeing is the problem when we try to graph politics onto religion or vice versa. And I think, you know, from my own --
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Saya pikir masalah yang kita lihat adalah ketika kita mencoba memetakan politik ke agama, atau sebaliknya. Dan saya pikir, Anda tahu, dari saya sendiri --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: No, that's very American.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, itu sangat Amerika.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: It is very American, but I just think -- fine, and people founded the country were proud Christians and I understand that, but the problem is that when churches start wading into politics, my own Catholicism when there are certain aspects of American politics that tend to come up much more in Catholic churches than others. You know, I don't -- I did not hear priests talking as much about war as they did about abortion and I understand that it's a big political issue.
paragraphs.cn: Memang sangat Amerika, tapi saya hanya berpikir -- yah, para pendiri negara ini adalah orang Kristen yang bangga, saya mengerti itu, tetapi masalahnya adalah ketika gereja mulai terlibat dalam politik, dalam iman Katolik saya sendiri, beberapa aspek politik Amerika lebih sering disebutkan di gereja Katolik daripada yang lain. Anda tahu, saya tidak -- saya tidak mendengar pendeta berbicara tentang perang sesering mereka berbicara tentang aborsi, dan saya mengerti itu adalah masalah politik besar.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Maybe the age you came up in too, right?
paragraphs.cn: Mungkin juga terkait dengan era Anda tumbuh dewasa, kan?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And, obviously, like certainly, civil rights era. There was a lot of pushback --
paragraphs.cn: Dan, tentu saja, seperti era hak-hak sipil, ada banyak suara yang menentang --

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: But immigration is the real central issue here.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi imigrasi adalah inti sebenarnya di sini.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: This the central issue of this administration, and Catholic teaching on this is pretty clear, which is why you have so many Catholic --
paragraphs.cn: Ini adalah masalah inti bagi pemerintahan ini, dan ajaran Katolik tentang hal ini sangat jelas, itulah mengapa ada begitu banyak umat Katolik --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It depends on what doctrine you're following.
paragraphs.cn: Itu tergantung pada ajaran mana yang Anda ikuti.

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: But, actually, I want to -- I want to -- on this point of the question of the tone.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi sebenarnya, saya ingin -- saya ingin -- mengenai masalah nada ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: I think if we go back to what J.D. Vance was saying about what the U.S. Conference of Catholic Bishops was criticizing the Trump administration for --
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir, jika kita melihat kembali komentar J.D. Vance tentang kritik Konferensi Waligereja Katolik Amerika Serikat terhadap kebijakan imigrasi pemerintahan Trump --

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: -- on immigration and JD Vance hit back on them and saying that they're essentially advocating --
paragraphs.cn: -- Vance membalasnya, mengatakan bahwa mereka sebenarnya menganjurkan --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Making money off of migrants.
paragraphs.cn: Mencari keuntungan dari imigran.

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: -- they're making money and advocating for this because they make money off it. I think that's extremely -- it was extremely uncharitable at the time, and I think that's what Ross is pressing him on. He's pressing him on the way that he's speaking about people who disagree with the administration is itself what is unchristian and I think that's what's -- what's most important from -- not from a necessarily political point of view but from a from a faith point of view.
paragraphs.cn: -- mereka menganjurkan hal-hal itu karena mereka mendapat keuntungan darinya. Saya pikir itu sangat tidak toleran saat itu, dan Ross menekannya pada titik itu. Dia mempertanyakan apakah cara dia berbicara tentang orang-orang yang tidak setuju dengan pemerintah itu sendiri tidak Kristen, dan saya pikir itulah yang paling penting -- tidak selalu dari sudut pandang politik, tetapi dari sudut pandang iman.

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: It's how we talk to -- about people who disagree with us.
paragraphs.cn: Intinya adalah bagaimana kita berbicara tentang orang-orang yang tidak setuju dengan kita.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Because people are talking about this in the context of 2028 as well this book tour.
paragraphs.cn: Karena orang juga membahas tur buku baru ini dalam konteks pemilihan 2028.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Yeah. It's like rebranding.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, ini seperti rebranding.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So the idea is if you're going to do a rebrand then people are probably going to look -- they're going to kick the tires on that --
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, jika Anda akan melakukan rebranding, orang kemungkinan akan mencermati --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: --and find some inconsistencies if you're fronting with your values driven policy.
paragraphs.cn: -- jika Anda mempromosikan diri dengan kebijakan yang berorientasi pada nilai, akan ditemukan beberapa inkonsistensi.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: One of the responses to this has gotten a lot of traction specifically on the right and the left was him sort of pushing back and saying that the language that -- that some of the language that's used is class-based, right, and that the elite spaces are pushing back on this language because they're not used to the rough and tumble of the F-bombs being deployed which -- and, you know, those on the right and the left have looked at this and said, like, this could have been, like, a hard left response to some of
paragraphs.cn: Salah satu tanggapan terhadap hal ini mendapat perhatian luas di kalangan kiri dan kanan, yaitu dia membantah bahwa beberapa istilah didasarkan pada kelas, dan bahwa kaum elit menentang istilah-istilah itu karena mereka tidak terbiasa dengan kata-kata kotor yang kasar -- dan orang-orang dari kiri dan kanan berpikir bahwa ini mungkin merupakan tanggapan sayap kiri ekstrem terhadap beberapa masalah.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: these issues.
paragraphs.cn: Masalah-masalah itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: It was a horseshoe there.
paragraphs.cn: Itu adalah sepatu kuda.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: It was a horseshoe thing, but even so, it elided some of the responsibility to this. All politicians have to respond to how they deploy language.
paragraphs.cn: Itu adalah sesuatu seperti sepatu kuda, tapi meskipun begitu, itu tetap mengelak dari tanggung jawab. Semua politisi harus merespons bagaimana mereka menggunakan bahasa.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I see, I see. It took me a minute. So, basically, he was saying, like, look, just the fact that you're asking this question reveals your kind of elitism, but y'all aren't used to the rough-and-tumble language of the hillbilly elegy and everyone's like but.
paragraphs.cn: Saya mengerti, saya mengerti. Butuh waktu sebentar bagi saya untuk menyadarinya. Jadi, pada dasarnya, dia mengatakan, lihat, pertanyaanmu sendiri sudah menunjukkan elitismemu, tapi kalian tidak terbiasa dengan bahasa kasar 'Hillbilly Elegy', dan kemudian semua orang berkata, tapi -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: I find that so condescending frankly --
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir itu merendahkan, terus terang -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: -- and acting as if he can speak for how working class people accept you know what's acceptable to them.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- dan dia seolah bisa mewakili apa yang diterima kelas pekerja, Anda tahu, apa yang bisa diterima bagi mereka.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, but that's also been part of his brand for a long time.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, tapi ini sudah lama menjadi bagian dari citranya.

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: Yeah. It's very, very uncharitable and I would say unchristian --
paragraphs.cn: Ya, ini sangat sangat tidak toleran, saya akan katakan tidak Kristiani -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right, I want to talk about another thing, which Jay Leno, you may remember him, absolute cold take. Late night TV is dead, he says. So, in an interview with 'Deadline", the former late night host said, quote, "I mean, podcasts really are the new talk shows. Joe Rogan is the new Johnny Carson." And he credited this to Rogan not having to deal with the FCC. And a younger generation, honestly, not that interested in watching TV and instead turning to YouTube. So the group chat is back.
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah, saya ingin membicarakan hal lain, Jay Leno, Anda mungkin ingat dia, pandangannya benar-benar ketinggalan zaman. Dia bilang TV larut malam sudah mati. Dalam wawancara dengan Deadline, mantan pembawa acara larut malam itu berkata: "Maksud saya, podcast adalah talk show yang baru. Joe Rogan adalah Johnny Carson yang baru." Dia mengaitkan ini dengan fakta bahwa Rogan tidak harus berurusan dengan FCC. Dan generasi muda, sejujurnya, tidak terlalu tertarik menonton TV, beralih ke YouTube. Jadi grup chat kembali lagi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I thought this was interesting because people have been using poor -- Joe Rogan as a proxy for every ill around the death of, I think, monoculture. The idea that how did this person sort of rise? The left wants a Joe Rogan. The right wants to hold on to a Joe Rogan. The late night guys are like, well, wait a second. Did I have to wear a tie? Can you talk about why you think this is such a deal?
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir ini menarik, karena orang terus menggunakan Joe Rogan yang malang sebagai kambing hitam untuk semua masalah, menurut saya, ini adalah matinya budaya tunggal. Bagaimana orang ini bangkit? Kiri menginginkan Joe Rogan. Kanan ingin merebut Joe Rogan. Pembawa acara larut malam berkata, tunggu, apakah saya harus memakai dasi? Bisakah Anda jelaskan mengapa Anda pikir ini penting?

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Think about one of the enduring questions out of the 2024 election. Should Kamala Harris have gone on Joe Rogan? Why did that not happen? And it's because of that Johnny Carson point. We are now in a world in which YouTube is king. We were talking a little bit earlier about books in the program, about how TikTok sells books. It is just a different world than the one that any of us grew up in, and it almost makes sense to get on board rather than try to fight it.
paragraphs.cn: Pikirkan tentang isu yang bertahan dalam pemilu 2024. Haruskah Kamala Harris tampil di acara Joe Rogan? Kenapa tidak jadi? Itu semua karena pandangan ala Johnny Carson. Kita sekarang berada di dunia di mana YouTube berkuasa. Kami sebelumnya membahas buku di acara ini, bagaimana TikTok menjual buku. Ini berbeda dari dunia tempat kita tumbuh, dan lebih masuk akal untuk bergabung daripada melawannya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I want to show you guys just some images of the talk shows that are doing well. You're going to be familiar with, like, Hot Ones or The Therapist, Chicken Shop Date, Subway Takes, Royal Court. These are all shows that are bite-sized. A lot of people take them in, in their virality. But I'm also interested in the fact that, frankly, they're longer than your average late-night show. Like, their ability to talk and hang out for a while feels like maybe that's what people are responding to?

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: I mean, as the host of an interview show where, you know, you do sit down with people for an extended period of time, it's just a different beast. It's a different animal. It -- you know, you nowadays have to make a show and have it go everywhere all the time, you know, on all the different platforms. And so --
paragraphs.cn: Sebagai pembawa acara talk show, Anda tahu, Anda duduk dengan orang-orang untuk waktu yang lama, itu hal yang sama sekali berbeda. Sekarang, Anda harus membuat acara yang bisa muncul di mana saja di semua platform berbeda. Jadi -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Because it's replacing broadcast.
paragraphs.cn: Karena ini menggantikan siaran televisi.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Because it's replacing broadcast. But the way people consume it, you know, people are going to see it on their TikTok feed, on their Instagram feed, and then if they like the clip, maybe, although --
paragraphs.cn: Karena ini menggantikan siaran televisi. Tapi cara orang mengonsumsinya, Anda tahu, orang akan melihatnya di feed TikTok, feed Instagram, dan jika mereka suka klipnya, mungkin, meskipun -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Versions aren't great, but maybe they'll go to YouTube. Maybe they'll sit down and watch the whole thing, but sometimes people are -- you know, people are sitting with these people, and they're getting to see them in a different way. If you're a long-form interviewer, the first 20 minutes are always, like, people getting comfortable, people --
paragraphs.cn: Versinya kurang ideal, tapi mungkin mereka akan pergi ke YouTube. Mungkin mereka akan duduk dan menonton episode penuh, tapi kadang orang -- Anda tahu, orang duduk dengan narasumber ini dan melihat mereka dengan cara berbeda. Jika Anda pembawa acara wawancara panjang, 20 menit pertama selalu untuk membuat orang rileks, membuat orang -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yeah. Can I show you the face of a long-form interviewer, okay? Because TV could have done this a lot longer than they did at Dick Cavett. It's a great example of like the period of time where people would do long winding political kind of going all over the place chats, which honestly could be a podcast that works today. Whereas I don't think you could have a Johnny Carson today because each one of these guys has picked out Elaine based on ideology.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Boleh saya tunjukkan wajah pembawa acara wawancara panjang? Karena TV sebenarnya bisa bertahan lebih lama dari era Dick Cavett. Ini contoh bagus bahwa di masa tertentu, orang melakukan obrolan politik yang panjang, berliku, dan imajinatif, yang sejujurnya bisa dijadikan podcast hari ini. Dan saya pikir tidak mungkin ada Johnny Carson lagi hari ini, karena setiap pembawa acara memilih tamu berdasarkan ideologi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Since there are no collective facts, you pick Elaine and you start poking jokes at the ideologies.
paragraphs.cn: Karena tidak ada fakta bersama, Anda memilih tamu dan kemudian mulai bercanda tentang ideologi.

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: I wonder if it's more technological, right? I mean, I think the point Jay Leno was trying to make here is that that sort of -- he was complaining actually about the way that ads kind of have broken more into late night and you know, it went from -- you know, a very --
paragraphs.cn: Saya ingin tahu apakah ini lebih karena teknologi, kan? Maksud saya, saya pikir yang ingin dikatakan Jay Leno adalah bahwa -- -- dia sebenarnya mengeluh tentang bagaimana iklan semakin menginvasi acara larut malam, Anda tahu, itu dari -- -- Anda tahu, yang sangat -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: Yeah, exactly, and that there's a lot more sort of freedom and people can sort of choose what they want to watch with these long- form interviews.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, benar, dan sekarang ada lebih banyak kebebasan, orang bisa memilih konten yang ingin mereka tonton melalui wawancara panjang ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: But is Call Your Daddy, is Hot Ones, is Subway Takes ideological?
paragraphs.cn: Tapi, apakah acara 'Call Your Daddy', 'Hot Ones', dan 'Subway Takes' memiliki kecenderungan ideologis?

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: I don't think so. So I don't know that that actually holds as a premise. I think --
paragraphs.cn: Saya rasa tidak. Jadi saya tidak yakin premis itu benar. Saya rasa -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: No. I mean, like a Jimmy Kimmel is, right? A Colbert was. They picked lanes.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, maksud saya, acara seperti Jimmy Kimmel punya, kan? Colbert juga. Mereka memilih jalannya sendiri.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: And they -- they picked lanes they did well by it, you know, in this very fragmented culture that we live in.
paragraphs.cn: Mereka memilih jalur yang mereka kuasai, Anda tahu, dalam budaya yang sangat terfragmentasi ini, mereka melakukannya dengan baik.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: I mean, if you look at what's replaced Colbert --
paragraphs.cn: Maksud saya, jika Anda lihat apa yang menggantikan Colbert -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I'm going to show the numbers now what's happened Colbert has left
paragraphs.cn: Saya akan tunjukkan datanya sekarang, apa yang terjadi setelah Colbert pergi

paragraphs.speaker: Cbs. Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: It hasn't done so well.
paragraphs.cn: Situasinya tidak terlalu baik.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right. Ellie, you haven't been here for a while. Can you tell me what's in your group chat?
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah, Ellie, kamu sudah lama tidak datang. Bisakah kamu ceritakan apa yang sedang dibicarakan di grup chatmu?

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: My group chat is about America. There's nothing more American than July 4th and eating hot dogs, and the criminal justice system, Joey Chestnut.
paragraphs.cn: Grup chat saya tentang Amerika. Tidak ada yang lebih Amerika dari 4 Juli, makan hot dog, sistem peradilan pidana, dan Joey Chestnut.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: That is the opening to Law and Order.
paragraphs.cn: Itu adalah pembukaan dari Law & Order.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Yes. Joey Chestnut, this champion hot dog eater, is on probation and will be able to compete on July 4th in the Nathan's Hot Dog Eating Contest from probation for an assault. God bless America.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Juara makan hot dog Joey Chestnut sedang dalam masa percobaan, dan dia bisa mengikuti kontes hot dog Nathan's pada 4 Juli karena hukuman percobaannya atas tuduhan penyerangan. God bless America.

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: There's nothing sacred.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak ada yang sakral.

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.en: We've been talking about this father who brought his two little girls into a gas station bathroom. He brought them into the women's restroom, and he began filming it because a man, another man, sort of broke in to say, there's a grown man in the women's restroom. The young father's trying to explain, I'm here to help my kids, to help my little girls. And it's just caused a lot of conversation.
paragraphs.cn: Kami sedang membicarakan tentang seorang ayah yang membawa kedua putrinya yang masih kecil ke toilet pom bensin. Dia membawa mereka ke toilet wanita dan mulai merekam, karena seorang pria, pria lain, tiba-tiba masuk dan berkata, ada pria dewasa di toilet wanita. Ayah muda itu mencoba menjelaskan, saya membantu anak saya, membantu putri kecil saya. Ini memicu banyak diskusi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I get it. It's a viral moment. Last word to you, Lulu.
paragraphs.cn: Saya mengerti. Ini adalah momen viral. Kata terakhir untukmu, Lulu.

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: My one is about a tree that is sadly RIP, 1,200-year- old tree that was there during the days of Robin Hood, 1,200 years old and is now, sadly, no longer alive.
paragraphs.cn: Saya akan berbicara tentang sebuah pohon, yang sayangnya sudah mati, pohon berusia 1200 tahun, sudah ada sejak zaman Robin Hood, 1200 tahun, dan sekarang sayangnya tidak hidup lagi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Okay, that is a timeline cleanser. Stay here. The headlines are next. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: Baik, ini adalah timeline cleanser. Tetap saksikan. Berikutnya adalah berita utama.

vocabulary.en: surrender without any conditions or demands

vocabulary.exampleCn: Trump mengklaim perjanjian Iran adalah 'penyerahan tanpa syarat'

vocabulary.en: a document describing a preliminary agreement between parties
vocabulary.excerpt: the MOU doesn't look like unconditional surrender
vocabulary.exampleCn: Nota kesepahaman ini tidak terlihat seperti penyerahan tanpa syarat

vocabulary.en: the blocking of a port or coastline by naval forces to prevent trade or military movement
vocabulary.excerpt: the naval blockade has been lifted
vocabulary.exampleCn: 海上封锁已经解除

vocabulary.cn: 霍尔木兹海峡（连接波斯湾与印度洋的战略水道）
vocabulary.en: a narrow strait between Iran and the Arabian Peninsula, crucial for global oil transport
vocabulary.excerpt: The Strait of Hormuz is technically open
vocabulary.exampleCn: 霍尔木兹海峡理论上已开放

vocabulary.en: minor difficulties or obstacles in a process
vocabulary.excerpt: I think we had to expect a lot of bumps in the road
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我认为我们必须预料到很多挫折

vocabulary.en: not clearly expressed or defined
vocabulary.excerpt: this is a very vague and very partial agreement
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这是一份非常模糊且不完整的协议

vocabulary.cn: 部分协议；不完整的协议
vocabulary.en: an agreement that covers only some aspects of a dispute
vocabulary.excerpt: this is a very vague and very partial agreement
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这是一份非常模糊且不完整的协议

vocabulary.en: to impose a legal or moral obligation
vocabulary.excerpt: it did not bind Israel to anything
vocabulary.exampleCn: 它没有对以色列施加任何约束

vocabulary.cn: 真主党（黎巴嫩什叶派政治和军事组织）
vocabulary.en: a Lebanese Shia Islamist political party and militant group
vocabulary.excerpt: It did not bind Hezbollah to anything
vocabulary.exampleCn: 它没有对真主党施加任何约束

vocabulary.en: an advantage or option that can be used in a situation
vocabulary.excerpt: Iran now has a card to play, which is peace in Lebanon
vocabulary.exampleCn: 伊朗现在有了一张可打的牌，那就是黎巴嫩的和平

vocabulary.en: an attack by military aircraft
vocabulary.excerpt: 16 people were killed after Israeli airstrikes
vocabulary.exampleCn: 以色列空袭造成16人死亡

vocabulary.en: to cause disagreement or conflict between groups
vocabulary.excerpt: it is an Iranian objective to sow divisions between the United States and Israel
vocabulary.exampleCn: 伊朗的目标是在美国和以色列之间制造分裂

vocabulary.en: to discourage someone from doing something through fear of consequences
vocabulary.excerpt: that rests on deterring what are very real threats to your people
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这依赖于威慑那些对人民构成的真实威胁

vocabulary.en: a non-binding agreement outlining the terms of a deal
vocabulary.excerpt: because there is now this memorandum of understanding with Iran
vocabulary.exampleCn: 因为现在有了这份与伊朗的谅解备忘录

vocabulary.cn: 神权疯子（贬义，指伊朗宗教领袖）
vocabulary.en: derogatory term for religious rulers considered irrational
vocabulary.excerpt: giving billions of dollars to theocratic lunatics who want to murder us
vocabulary.exampleCn: 把数十亿美元给那些想谋杀我们的神权疯子

vocabulary.en: a very small amount of money
vocabulary.excerpt: would make Obama's 2015 deal look like a pittance by comparison
vocabulary.exampleCn: 相比之下会让奥巴马2015年的协议显得微不足道

vocabulary.en: confusing or puzzling
vocabulary.excerpt: that's the one part of this thing that is the most mystifying at all
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这是整件事中最令人费解的部分

vocabulary.en: penalties imposed by one country on another to force compliance
vocabulary.excerpt: sanctions are being lifted
vocabulary.exampleCn: 制裁正在被解除

vocabulary.en: a pool of money designated for rebuilding infrastructure and economy
vocabulary.excerpt: the $300 billion potential reconstruction and development fund
vocabulary.exampleCn: 3000亿美元的潜在重建与发展基金

vocabulary.en: impossible to explain or understand
vocabulary.excerpt: That's kind of inexplicable
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这有点无法解释

vocabulary.en: legal obligation to comply
vocabulary.excerpt: has no binding effect on Israel or Hezbollah
vocabulary.exampleCn: 对以色列或真主党没有约束力
